Ephedra obvallata, also known as Ephedra, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Ephedraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis due to its bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra obvallata include ephedrine, pseudoephedrine, and other alkaloids that have been shown to have vasoconstrictive and bronchodilatory effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ephedra include extracts, tinctures, and capsules, often used to support respiratory health and as an energy booster.
